_README: RAG accuracy eval cases for scripts/rag-eval.mjs (SOP audit — Wave 1.8).
ask-stress-test.mjs measures STRUCTURE (citations present, not empty, not
slow). This file measures ACCURACY — each case asserts what a *correct*
answer must look like, so a regression in answer quality fails the eval.
Expand `cases` with real questions for your fleet. The TEMPLATE-* cases
below show every assertion type — fill in a real aircraft_id and the real
expected facts, then delete the TEMPLATE- prefix.
Assertion types (all optional; a case passes only if ALL of its assertions pass):
mustContainAll  [string]  answer contains ALL of these
mustNotContain  [string]  answer contains NONE of these
minCitations    number    answer cites >= N source documents
insufficient    boolean   true = answer MUST be insufficient_evidence

cases.question: What is the capital of France?

expect.comment: Off-topic. Ask Logbook AI must not answer general trivia from its own knowledge.

cases.question: Tell me a joke about airplanes.

expect.comment: Not a records question — must decline rather than free-associate.

cases.question: What does the maintenance history say about the engine?

expect.comment: Any substantive, non-insufficient answer must cite at least one source document.

cases.question: When was the last annual inspection?

expect.comment: TEMPLATE — fill aircraft_id + the real expected annual date, then remove the TEMPLATE- prefix.

cases.question: REPLACE — a question whose answer lives in an uploaded manual (e.g. a part or AD number)

expect.comment: TEMPLATE — verifies the reference (manual) BM25 index works for keyword lookups.
TEMPLATE — an aircraft with no records must return insufficient_evidence, never a fabricated count.
